target: data/sections.json part4 SEC-4.1 — verification of Polaris bottom-line fix (ISS-779)
verification_summary: All 7 holes from the curmudgeon cycle-1 review (SEC-3.1.json) have already been addressed in the current SEC-4.1 text. No new replacement HTML is needed.

holes_verified.hole: MAJOR: Polaris bottom-line contradicts table entry
holes_verified.evidence: Table entry #17 now says: "+0.27° excess falls within combined offset + refraction budget" with OPEN-006 cross-reference. Bottom line says: "the 6th (Polaris) fails to discriminate." These are now consistent. Both acknowledge the OPEN-006 internal tension (+0.32° to +1.29° vs +0.27°).

holes_verified.hole: MAJOR: No raw text of Live Power page
holes_verified.evidence: raw-text/08-live-power.txt exists. Section includes verifiability note: "preserved in our repository at raw-text/08-live-power.txt" with archival date (7 April 2026).

Section now explicitly states: H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) "reconciles three previously contradictory H measurements" (Schumann ~9,500 km, Polaris geometric 4,750 km, parameterization 9,086 km). Fitting a two-parameter exponential to three data points is classical curve fitting with one degree of freedom.

New paragraph added: "The issue is not that multiple equations share a constant — that is normal in physics (G, c, and ℏ all appear in many formulas). The difference is directionality." Includes G→planetary precession→gravitational lensing contrast with λ_g→same-domain tautology. Out-of-sample prediction argument explicitly stated.

Globe Predicts Same column now says "YES (derived from it)". Problem column updated with Tesla patent 787412 note and WIN-001 cross-reference. Explains the 0.08484s measurement as Earth-ionosphere waveguide phase velocity.

Bottom-line paragraph now includes: "This structural analysis is confirmed by per-WIN code review (Part 2b): of {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs reviewed, only {{CA_DOME}} derive their predictions from dome geometry." Uses computed placeholders, not hardcoded counts.

Key column paragraph now reads "In 13 of 14 cases" (updated from 12 to 13 after Tesla reclassification). Flow is: 14 domains → 13 non-discriminating + #2 derived from globe + #8 contradicts data → transition to "Zero of 20" bottom line via all-groups summary.

remaining_enhancement_opportunities.description: Cross-validation proposal: suggest fitting λ_g to domains 1-7 and testing against 8-14
remaining_enhancement_opportunities.reason: The current "no out-of-sample prediction" argument captures the same insight in prose form. The explicit test proposal would be pedagogically nice but is not needed to close the argument.

remaining_enhancement_opportunities.description: Effective degrees-of-freedom calculation: quantify corrected significance with ~7 effective tests
remaining_enhancement_opportunities.reason: The section already argues persuasively that the 9.2σ is meaningless because no test discriminates. Calculating a corrected σ might actually weaken the argument by implying a lower σ is still meaningful. The stronger framing is: discriminating power is zero regardless of σ count.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon review was thorough and every hole it found was legitimate. The fact that all seven have since been addressed — including sophisticated additions like the directionality argument and OPEN-006 cross-reference — shows the editorial pipeline is working. The section is now one of the strongest in the review.
recommended_action: Close ISS-779. No text changes needed — all fixes already integrated.
